paper_title: Overexpressed transient receptor potential vanilloid 3 ion channels in skin keratinocytes modulate pain sensitivity via prostaglandin E2.
key_table_figure_to_check: Check evidence locators in this card
gold_notes: This paper falls within the field of neurobiology/pain pharmacology. Rather than using a Franz diffusion cell to measure drug “permeability,” it employs **cell culture techniques (patch-clamp electrophysiology, molecular release assays) and in vivo behavioral studies (in vivo pain assessment)**. The record is same from R2-047 to R2-051.

paper_title: Compatibilizing of cotton fabric with hydrophobic drug cover layer for anti-inflammatory performance with the implementation of ibuprofen.
gold_endpoint_value_note: The dressing exhibited a pronounced “burst release” effect, releasing approximately $64\ \mu g$ of ibuprofen within the first 2 minutes. This was followed by a gradual sustained-release phase; after 14 days, the PLA-PEG carrier group (Sample C) had released a total of $209\ \mu g$, while the star-PLA carrier group (Sample E) had released $239\ \mu g$.
gold_notes: Instead of using a Franz diffusion cell to measure the rate of drug penetration through the skin, ibuprofen was loaded onto a modified cotton fabric, and the drug release kinetics, cytotoxicity, and anti-inflammatory activity of the dressing were tested. record is same from R2-052 to R2-061.

paper_title: Soluplus<sup></sup> polymeric nanomicelles improve solubility of BCS-class II drugs.

paper_title: SEBS block copolymers as novel materials to design transdermal patches.
gold_endpoint_value_note: The endpoint value states in Table 5, and there are six type of ibu-formulation and time is 24h.
gold_notes: This study falls within the field of transdermal drug delivery systems (specifically, innovations in pressure-sensitive adhesive materials for transdermal patches). It explores a novel polymer material—SEBS block copolymer—for use as the pressure-sensitive adhesive matrix in transdermal patches, and employs ibuprofen as a model drug to evaluate its release and transdermal performance. The drug loading of ibuprofen in the dried matrix of the medicated patch was 10% (w/w). The endpoint of the skin permeation study was 24 hours (24 h). The membrane used was pig ear skin.

paper_title: Fluorinated PAMAM-Arginine Carrier Prodrugs for pH-Sensitive Sustained Ibuprofen Delivery.
gold_endpoint_value_note: - Prodrug sustained release: At 72 hours, the cumulative release rate of the prodrug in an alkaline environment (pH 8) was close to 80%, whereas release was slower in a physiological environment (pH 7.5), reaching only about 50%.
gold_notes: This study falls under the field of Nanomedicine / Smart Prodrugs. Here, Ibuprofen is not only studied as an anti-inflammatory drug; it is also covalently linked to a fluorinated dendrimer, forming a “theranostic” prodrug system that can achieve sustained-release in response to pH and also has the potential to serve as a ${}^{19}F$ magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) contrast agent.

paper_title: Use of Silicone Membrane Permeation to Assess Thermodynamic Activities of Ionic Liquids and Their Component Cation and Anion.
api_concentration_raw: LID/IBU ratio 0/1.0 (at preparation); LID/IBU as IL 0/0; Dissolved LID/IBU 0/0; Suspended LID/IBU 0/1.0

gold_notes: In this study, ibuprofen, acting as an anion, was mixed with lidocaine (a cation) to form an ionic liquid (IL). Instead of using animal skin, the study employed a silicone membrane as a model, with the sole purpose of analyzing the diffusion behavior of the ionic liquid during transmembrane transport from the perspectives of mathematical equations and thermodynamic activity. The experiments tested mixtures with different molar ratios. The molar fraction of ibuprofen (X_{Ibu}) was set to multiple values ranging from 0 to 1.0 (e.g., 0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9, etc.).

gold_notes: In this study, ibuprofen, acting as an anion, was mixed with lidocaine (a cation) to form an ionic liquid (IL). Instead of using animal skin, the study employed a silicone membrane as a model, with the sole purpose of analyzing the diffusion behavior of the ionic liquid during transmembrane transport from the perspectives of mathematical equations and thermodynamic activity. The experiments tested mixtures with different molar ratios. The molar fraction of ibuprofen (X_{Ibu}) was set to multiple values ranging from 0 to 1.0 (e.g., 0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9, etc.).
